rationale: X's core-fix narrative (codes.audio vs legacy audio, #4527 gap, decode feedback silently dropped) almost exactly mirrors the ground-truth reviewer's approval rationale, giving it stronger recall on the substantive point; its two non-blocking comments are grounded and concretely actionable. Y produces more findings with precise file:line evidence and slightly better actionability, but they skew toward low-value/nitpicky defensive suggestions (unpopped legacy key, missing IndexError guard, duplicate magic-number comments) that don't engage with the ground truth's central concern, and two of its five comments are redundant restatements of the same magic-number issue.
